description: @trial_mix for Eckard & Kyonka (2018) peak procedure: FI18s LH3s baseline, 45 FI trials + 15 peak trials (54s, unreinforced) per session
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=1, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s, ordering_spec="random")

Mix(FI18s LH3s, EXT)
_note: Actual Eckard & Kyonka (2018) parameters: FI 18s with 3s limited hold, probe duration 54s (3x FI value). Mix has 2 components: reinforced FI (ref=0) and extinguished probe (ref=1). Session composition: 45 reinforced + 15 peak, randomly intermixed. Runtime seed/shuffling delegated to execution_profile.

description: @trial_mix for Roberts (1981) classical peak procedure: FI30s baseline, 90s probes, balanced counts
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=30, probe_ref=1, probe_count=10, probe_duration=90s)

Mix(FI30s, EXT)
_note: Roberts (1981) 'Isolation of an internal clock' — canonical peak procedure. probe_duration 90s = 3x FI 30s. ordering_spec defaults to 'random' per schema.

description: @trial_mix applied to Mult (discriminated peak procedure with stimulus cueing)
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=40, probe_ref=1, probe_count=20, probe_duration=60s, ordering_spec="random")

Mult(FI20s, EXT)
_note: Mult (discriminated) peak: distinct SDs for reinforced and probe components enable stimulus control analysis.

description: @trial_mix for Baker et al. (1991) reinstatement: extinction + 4 response-independent reinforcer deliveries on FT60s
input:
@trial_mix(type="reinstatement", extinction_ref=0, reinstatement_ref=1, reinstatement_count=4, reinstatement_interval=60s, ordering_spec="fixed_sequence")

Mix(EXT, FT60s)
_note: Baker, Steinwald & Bouton (1991) instrumental reinstatement: 4 response-independent reinforcers on FT60s interleaved with EXT background. ordering_spec='fixed_sequence' because reinstatement is typically deterministic (reinforcers at specific timings).

description: @trial_mix reinstatement with interval omitted (derived from reinstatement_ref's FT parameter)
_note: reinstatement_interval omitted: runtime infers 120s from FT120s component. ordering_spec defaults to 'random' per schema (linter may still warn given reinstatement typically uses 'fixed_sequence').

description: @trial_mix for Church & Deluty (1977) temporal bisection (free-operant variant): 2s short anchor, 8s long anchor, 5 intermediate probes logarithmically spaced
input:
@trial_mix(type="temporal_bisection", short_anchor_ref=0, short_anchor_duration=2s, short_anchor_count=20, long_anchor_ref=1, long_anchor_duration=8s, long_anchor_count=20, probe_ref=2, probe_count=10, probe_duration_list=["2.52s", "3.17s", "4s", "5.04s", "6.35s"], ordering_spec="random")

Mix(FI2s, FI8s, EXT)
_note: Church & Deluty (1977) used 5 probe durations logarithmically spaced between 2s and 8s: 2*(4)^(k/6) for k=1..5. Fetterman & Killeen (1995) showed free-operant variants generate similar psychophysical functions. probe_count=10 means 10 trials per probe duration (total 50 probe trials + 40 anchor trials = 90 per session).

input:
@trial_mix(reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=1, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s)

Mix(FI18s LH3s, EXT)
_note: type parameter is required; must be one of 'peak', 'reinstatement', 'temporal_bisection'.

description: @trial_mix peak with identical reinforced_ref and probe_ref triggers TRIAL_MIX_DUPLICATE_REF
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=0, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s)

Mix(FI18s, EXT)
_note: Component refs must reference distinct components.

description: @trial_mix with ref beyond Mix component count triggers TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_REF
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=5, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s)

Mix(FI18s, EXT)
_note: probe_ref=5 indexes beyond Mix's 2 components (indices 0 and 1).

input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=1, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s)

MTS(samples=3, comparisons=3)
_note: MTS is Core-TrialBased (discrete-trial). For MTS probe testing, use @testing (procedure-stimulus) instead. This separation enforces the layer boundary established by Theorem 1 (core-trial-based/theory.md §4).

description: @trial_mix type=peak with reinstatement-only param triggers TRIAL_MIX_FORBIDDEN_PARAM
input:
@trial_mix(type="peak", reinforced_ref=0, reinforced_count=45, probe_ref=1, probe_count=15, probe_duration=54s, extinction_ref=0, reinstatement_count=4)

Mix(FI18s, EXT)
_note: extinction_ref and reinstatement_count are reinstatement-only per schema forbidden_if. Linter must reject mixing peak fields with reinstatement fields.

input:
@trial_mix(type="temporal_bisection", short_anchor_ref=0, short_anchor_duration=2s, short_anchor_count=20, long_anchor_ref=1, long_anchor_duration=8s, long_anchor_count=20, probe_ref=1, probe_count=10, probe_duration_list=["4s"])

Mix(FI2s, FI8s)
_note: temporal_bisection requires >=3 components (short anchor, long anchor, probe); the given Mix has only 2 components.

description: @trial_mix temporal_bisection with probe duration outside [short, long] range triggers TRIAL_MIX_PROBE_OUT_OF_ANCHOR_RANGE
input:
@trial_mix(type="temporal_bisection", short_anchor_ref=0, short_anchor_duration=2s, short_anchor_count=20, long_anchor_ref=1, long_anchor_duration=8s, long_anchor_count=20, probe_ref=2, probe_count=10, probe_duration_list=["1s", "4s", "10s"])

Mix(FI2s, FI8s, EXT)
_note: probe_duration_list entries '1s' (< 2s short anchor) and '10s' (> 8s long anchor) are out of range. Probes must be strictly between short_anchor_duration and long_anchor_duration.

description: @trial_mix temporal_bisection with malformed time string triggers TRIAL_MIX_INVALID_PROBE_DURATION
input:
@trial_mix(type="temporal_bisection", short_anchor_ref=0, short_anchor_duration=2s, short_anchor_count=20, long_anchor_ref=1, long_anchor_duration=8s, long_anchor_count=20, probe_ref=2, probe_count=10, probe_duration_list=["three_seconds", "4s"])

Mix(FI2s, FI8s, EXT)
_note: 'three_seconds' is not a parseable time_value string. Valid forms: '3s', '150ms', '1min'.
